message:
fix(durability): fsync sidecar writes to prevent silent data loss on power failure

file_protector.rs was using bare fs::write() for repair sidecar files,
which does not guarantee durability — the kernel may buffer the data and
a power loss before writeback would silently lose the sidecar, leaving
the protected file without its RaptorQ repair symbols despite
create_sidecar() having returned Ok.

Now writes through a File handle with explicit write_all + sync_all,
ensuring the sidecar data reaches stable storage before returning
success to the caller.

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl with latest concurrent agent work
(self-update pipeline security hardening, ULID telemetry IDs, and
additional issue tracker updates).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
